architecture.summary: This paper investigates multi-dimensional feature representations in language models (GPT-2, Mistral 7B, Llama 3 8B) using sparse autoencoders (SAEs) to decompose hidden states. Information flows through: (1) SAE dictionary learning to over-complete basis representations, (2) cosine-similarity clustering of dictionary elements to identify candidate multi-dimensional features, (3) PCA projection and statistical tests (separability index, ε-mixture index) to verify irreducibility, and (4) activation patching interventions on discovered circular subspaces to establish causal role in modular arithmetic tasks. The system reads activation space and outputs identified multi-dimensional feature manifolds (circles, cones) without modifying model parameters.

biblio.title: Not All Language Model Features Are One-Dimensionally Linear

claims.statement: Language models contain irreducible multi-dimensional features that cannot be decomposed into independent or non-co-occurring lower-dimensional features, contrary to the one-dimensional linear representation hypothesis.

Sparse autoencoders with cosine-similarity clustering can automatically discover interpretable multi-dimensional circular features representing days of the week, months of the year, and years in GPT-2 and Mistral 7B.
Mistral 7B achieves 31/49 accuracy on weekdays modular arithmetic task and 125/144 on months task.
Llama 3 8B achieves 29/49 accuracy on weekdays task and 143/144 on months task.
Circular subspace intervention on Mistral 7B achieves nearly the same causal effect as patching the entire layer, with average logit difference approaching layer-patching baselines.
Off-distribution interventions sweeping within circular representations show that models treat the circle as a multi-dimensional representation with task variable encoded in the angle.
SAE-discovered circular features in layer 8 Mistral 7B are more robust to layer shifts than PCA-trained circular probes, suggesting SAE findings capture more fundamental model features.
Mistral 7B represents continuous time by mapping intermediate weekday descriptors (very early/very late, morning/evening) to expected positions along the circular day-of-week representation.
